Shared artifacts: short deck link (internal): https://rwd.company/share/nimbus-microbench.pdf, bench sheet: /redwood/benchmarks/nimbus-pilot.xlsx

Summary:
Quick technical deep dive to align on microbench scope: compare TensorRT-LLM (TRT-LLM) cudagraph-enabled runs vs vLLM optimized runtime under hybrid quant profiles, verify KV cache behavior across sequential batches, and validate VPC/private-cluster connectivity for a 3-day pilot. Customer cares about 95th latency, tail stability under burst traffic, and on-prem constraints (HSM/KMS).

Topics:
- Test matrix: 7B/13B/50B, seq len 512/2048, batch sizes 1/4/8
- Quant profile...

title: 2027-08-01-precision-contrast-lab-demo
preview: Optimize Console walkthrough focused on contrast between INT8 and FP8 precision profiles. Demo included console QProfile selector, live benches showing tail latency and throughput, and discussion of fallback rules and quality regressions. Customer concerned about hallucination risk and latency spikes during cold cac...
text: Optimize Console walkthrough focused on contrast between INT8 and FP8 precision profiles. Demo included console QProfile selector, live benches showing tail latency and throughput, and discussion of fallback rules and quality regressions. Customer concerned about hallucination risk and latency spikes during cold cache. Agreed next steps to run pilot in sandbox and exchange sample workload traces.

title: Miridian - POC weekly latency & throughput review
preview: Weekly POC sync covering lab benchmark deltas vs the customer's incumbent provider. Redwood showed lower median token latency and similar throughput under steady load but higher 95th percentile on one traffic pattern; discussed batching, quantization tradeoffs, and KV/prefix caching. Agreed to run tail latency sweep...
text: Weekly POC sync covering lab benchmark deltas vs the customer's incumbent provider. Redwood showed lower median token latency and similar throughput under steady load but higher 95th percentile on one traffic pattern; discussed batching, quantization tradeoffs, and KV/prefix caching. Agreed to run tail latency sweeps, share config/benchmark sheet, and schedule a deeper routing/quantization deep-dive next week.

title: Long context + high TPS stress walkthrough - Redwood demo
preview: Redwood AE/SE walk through an edge-case focused demo simulating long conversation contexts, high TPS, and streaming backpressure. Customer is evaluating hosted vs dedicated for a real-time analytics assistant. Key asks: tail-latency under load, graceful streaming reconnects, quantization/kv cache effects on long con...
text: Redwood AE/SE walk through an edge-case focused demo simulating long conversation contexts, high TPS, and streaming backpressure. Customer is evaluating hosted vs dedicated for a real-time analytics assistant. Key asks: tail-latency under load, graceful streaming reconnects, quantization/kv cache effects on long context, and SLA pricing for bursty traffic.

title: Moonlit Devs — Onramp timing and privacy considerations
preview: Discovery call focused on Moonlit Devs' need to get to first API call quickly while evaluating privacy (VPC/on-prem) requirements and whether to start hosted with free credits or pursue a Dedicated/private pilot. Key concerns: data residency, KMS, audit logs, latency SLOs for inference, and cost tradeoffs between ho...
text: Discovery call focused on Moonlit Devs' need to get to first API call quickly while evaluating privacy (VPC/on-prem) requirements and whether to start hosted with free credits or pursue a Dedicated/private pilot. Key concerns: data residency, KMS, audit logs, latency SLOs for inference, and cost tradeoffs between hosted vs. dedicated. Agreed next steps include security docs, free credits, and a scoped pilot to run benchmarks.

title: Marketplace onboarding walkthrough (internal dry run) — subscription → org provisioning → first key
preview: Internal walkthrough of the end-to-end marketplace onboarding flow to validate the UX and docs before submission. Team identified confusion around org linking for existing Redwood orgs, unclear messaging during entitlement propagation delays (AWS especially), and missing docs for the "already subscribed" and "multip...
text: Internal walkthrough of the end-to-end marketplace onboarding flow to validate the UX and docs before submission. Team identified confusion around org linking for existing Redwood orgs, unclear messaging during entitlement propagation delays (AWS especially), and missing docs for the "already subscribed" and "multiple subscriptions" edge cases. Engineering confirmed expected entitlement API error codes and noted where console should map them to actionable retry guidance. Docs team flagged needed screenshots and a clearer separation of AWS vs GCP steps, plus a single canonical "How marketplace billing maps to usage" section to reduce support tickets.

Team aligned on a conservative ramp, a hard exclusion list refresh cadence, and how to interpret activation vs retention signals for retargeting.

title: DevFlow AI x Redwood - onboarding feedback / first request friction
preview: DevFlow AI shared onboarding friction from a recent self-serve trial of Redwood Hosted API. Main blockers were: confusion on where to create an API key + which org/project it belongs to, docs/quickstart mismatch with current SDK methods, unclear model name selection, and lack of in-console “next step” guidance after...
text: DevFlow AI shared onboarding friction from a recent self-serve trial of Redwood Hosted API. Main blockers were: confusion on where to create an API key + which org/project it belongs to, docs/quickstart mismatch with current SDK methods, unclear model name selection, and lack of in-console “next step” guidance after signup. They also hit a 401 due to using a revoked key and didn’t notice the error details; wanted a clearer error surface and copyable curl/SDK snippets in the console. Team uses Node/TypeScript and Vercel; they care about low-latency streaming for an “AI pair programmer” feature and want predictable spend. Requested: onboarding checklist in console, a single minimal “first request” path, clearer rate limit/quotas visibility, and a debugging page for common errors (401/429/model not found).

title: Weekly Growth Experiments Readout (Lifecycle sprint) - Jan 21
preview: Team reviewed early Week 1 results for onboarding email v2 A/B (opens/clicks and preliminary activation lift), agreed to keep test running through end of week while fixing a small tracking discrepancy. Product/Eng walked through Week 2 console nudge rollout: targeted checklist-style nudges (API key creation + first ...
text: Team reviewed early Week 1 results for onboarding email v2 A/B (opens/clicks and preliminary activation lift), agreed to keep test running through end of week while fixing a small tracking discrepancy. Product/Eng walked through Week 2 console nudge rollout: targeted checklist-style nudges (API key creation + first request) behind feature flags with a holdout group, plus QA and dismissal behavior fixes before ramping exposure.

title: GCP Marketplace onboarding + billing review (Redwood Inference)
preview: Redwood and the GCP Marketplace team reviewed how Redwood’s new marketplace SKUs map to GCP billing dimensions (usage-based token/embedding meters vs subscription/commit dimensions), and what identifiers must be carried end-to-end (consumerId/entitlement) to ensure correct metering. GCP emphasized keeping dimension ...
text: Redwood and the GCP Marketplace team reviewed how Redwood’s new marketplace SKUs map to GCP billing dimensions (usage-based token/embedding meters vs subscription/commit dimensions), and what identifiers must be carried end-to-end (consumerId/entitlement) to ensure correct metering. GCP emphasized keeping dimension names stable, ensuring aggregation and rounding are consistent, and making the onboarding flow resilient to entitlement propagation delays. The group aligned on a staging test plan: subscribe with a test buyer account, validate entitlement lookup, provision/link a Redwood org, generate an API key, send test inference traffic, confirm metering events appear with the expected dimensions, and verify cancellation/plan change behavior. Redwood will send a proposed dimension list and sample metering payloads for review.

title: Lifecycle growth experiments sprint kickoff (Hosted API activation/retention)
preview: Kickoff for 4-week lifecycle growth experiments sprint focused on self-serve Hosted API activation + early retention. Team aligned on primary activation KPIs (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7 days), experiment tracks (email drip v2, in-console nudges, paid retargeting), instrumentation/event tax...
text: Kickoff for 4-week lifecycle growth experiments sprint focused on self-serve Hosted API activation + early retention. Team aligned on primary activation KPIs (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7 days), experiment tracks (email drip v2, in-console nudges, paid retargeting), instrumentation/event taxonomy, and guardrails (deliverability, CAN-SPAM/GDPR, sales opp exclusions, and experiment integrity). Owners confirmed and week-by-week deliverables reviewed.

Auto-summary (auto-generated, rough): NovusMed is evaluating dedicated capacity vs hosted Redwood for inference. Key concerns: data isolation, burst capacity during daily peak, predictable latency for SLAs, and cost. They want a pilot reserved pool, clear failover/fallbacks to hosted or degraded configs, and security proofs (SOC2, KMS integration). Action items and pricing required.

Topics: dedicated reservation sizing, isolation and tenancy (dedicated vs VPC), burst strategy (pre-warm, burst to hosted, queue/backpressure), scaling expectations, benchmark plan, compliance requirements.

title: Post-launch retro - Benchmark report campaign (KPI review + refresh plan)
preview: Team reviewed performance of the public benchmark report launch (landing page + interactive table + PDF + blog + webinar). Results were strong top-of-funnel with meaningful sales-assisted pipeline influence, but launch-day reliability issues (cache miss storm + 429s) created support load and some negative chatter. T...
text: Team reviewed performance of the public benchmark report launch (landing page + interactive table + PDF + blog + webinar). Results were strong top-of-funnel with meaningful sales-assisted pipeline influence, but launch-day reliability issues (cache miss storm + 429s) created support load and some negative chatter. The methodology page and reproducibility artifacts were consistently cited as the trust driver. Next phase focuses on (1) tightening follow-up loops for high-intent leads, (2) shipping a lightweight “replicate this in your PoC” guide and a clearer metric explainer, (3) improving cache/CDN settings and guardrails for future spikes, and (4) establishing a refresh cadence with clear material-change thresholds and legal review gates.

title: Redwood x SkyForge Cloud — benchmark report co-marketing sync (embargo + newsletter)
preview: Redwood and SkyForge aligned on a co-marketing plan for Redwood’s public benchmark report launch. SkyForge will place a newsletter blurb in the July issue and optionally amplify via marketplace/social, contingent on receiving final copy + a compliant “how we measured” snippet and approved visuals under embargo. Redw...
text: Redwood and SkyForge aligned on a co-marketing plan for Redwood’s public benchmark report launch. SkyForge will place a newsletter blurb in the July issue and optionally amplify via marketplace/social, contingent on receiving final copy + a compliant “how we measured” snippet and approved visuals under embargo. Redwood will provide a partner-ready copy block with conservative claims (no “fastest overall” language), a co-branded image, UTM-tagged links, and an embargoed briefing doc. Both teams agreed to a 24–48 hour approval buffer and a single source-of-truth for the headline metrics and disclaimers.

title: ABM pilot kickoff - regulated verticals (finserv + healthcare)
preview: Internal kickoff for the 6–8 week vertical ABM pilot targeting regulated workloads. Team aligned on primary goals (validate repeatable positioning + outbound motion, ship durable vertical assets, generate qualified pipeline for Dedicated/Private). Discussed whether to start with financial services only vs running fi...
text: Internal kickoff for the 6–8 week vertical ABM pilot targeting regulated workloads. Team aligned on primary goals (validate repeatable positioning + outbound motion, ship durable vertical assets, generate qualified pipeline for Dedicated/Private). Discussed whether to start with financial services only vs running finserv + healthcare in parallel; decision: prioritize finserv for weeks 1–4, keep healthcare assets in draft and pull forward if bandwidth allows / if we get warm signals. Reviewed asset plan (landing page, one-pagers, security FAQ + evidence pack index, outbound sequences, deck/battlecard, webinar outline, metrics dashboard), named owners, and set weekly cadence. Flagged key risks: compliance-safe claims (avoid “HIPAA-ready”), security questionnaire turnaround time, limited vertical proof points/logos, and inconsistent packaging guidance between Dedicated vs Private. Closed...

title: BluePeak AI - quickstart + first production launch checklist demo
preview: Redwood demoed the in-Console onboarding + “first production launch” checklist prototype and walked BluePeak AI through the updated Hosted API quickstart (streaming chat, retries/timeouts, and observability basics). BluePeak’s main concerns were around quota/rate-limit surprises, cost controls (hard budget caps vs a...
text: Redwood demoed the in-Console onboarding + “first production launch” checklist prototype and walked BluePeak AI through the updated Hosted API quickstart (streaming chat, retries/timeouts, and observability basics). BluePeak’s main concerns were around quota/rate-limit surprises, cost controls (hard budget caps vs alerts), and how quickly usage dashboards reflect spend. Redwood clarified quota vs rate limits, pointed to budget alert options and recommended client-side backoff patterns, and proposed next steps: enable a sandbox tenant for BluePeak, share the checklist doc links and a production readiness quickstart, and schedule a follow-up focused on quotas/budgets and rollout/fallback policies.

title: Lattice Search — security + production readiness review (Hosted API + roadmap)
preview: Lattice Search walked Redwood through their internal go-live/security approval requirements for adding an LLM inference provider to an enterprise search product (customer data, regulated customers). Key asks: auditability (who did what / when), SSO/SAML + RBAC, data retention and residency clarity, and a concrete “l...
text: Lattice Search walked Redwood through their internal go-live/security approval requirements for adding an LLM inference provider to an enterprise search product (customer data, regulated customers). Key asks: auditability (who did what / when), SSO/SAML + RBAC, data retention and residency clarity, and a concrete “launch checklist” they can attach to their change-management packet. Redwood clarified Hosted API data handling (no prompt storage by default; limited logs), available security controls today (SOC 2, encryption, key mgmt options depending on deployment), and what’s on the near-term roadmap (richer audit logs in Console, admin controls). Discussion also covered production readiness items: retries/timeouts, rate limiting/quotas, streaming disconnect behavior, and model pinning/fallback. Agreed to share Redwood’s security artifacts + draft production launch checklist, and sched...

title: Northwind AI - usage discrepancy escalation sync
Northstar Support Platform - onboarding friction + production readiness feedback
preview: Northstar shared detailed friction points from their first-week Hosted API trial: confusion around which API key to use and env var naming leading to 401/403, uncertainty on what a "successful" first request should look like (non-streaming vs streaming), surprise 429s when they switched from a curl test to a small l...
text: Northstar shared detailed friction points from their first-week Hosted API trial: confusion around which API key to use and env var naming leading to 401/403, uncertainty on what a "successful" first request should look like (non-streaming vs streaming), surprise 429s when they switched from a curl test to a small load test, and confusion when usage dashboards lagged. They asked Redwood to provide clearer "production readiness" steps (timeouts/retries, idempotency guidance, streaming reconnect, quotas/budget alerts, model pinning/fallback) and requested these be surfaced in Console rather than scattered across docs.

text:
Meeting header
Date: 2025-01-17
Start time: 10:00 AM PT
Duration: 44 min
Attendees: Ava Chen (Redwood), Ben Carter (Redwood), Nina Shah (BluePeak), Tom Wilkes (BluePeak), Priya Menon (BluePeak)

Summary
BluePeak is interested in Redwood Private but requires the deployment to run without outbound internet access ("no egress").

Transcript (excerpt)
Ava Chen: thanks for making time. can you describe the environment + any hard constraints?
Nina Shah: regulated environment. main thing is it's disconnected. no outbound internet. basically no egress.
Ben Carter: when you say no egress, is that enforced at the cluster level?
Tom Wilkes: yes. we can do internal traffic. but no public internet routes from the cluster.
Ava Chen: ok. then we would mirror images into your internal registry and avoid any external checks.
Nina Shah: yep. and we don't allow outbound OCSP/CRL either.

text:
Meeting Header
Date: 2024-11-12

Duration: 41 min
Attendees: Ben Carter (Redwood), Priya Natarajan (Redwood), Samantha Yuen (Northbridge Health), Miguel Ortega (Northbridge Health), Rina Shah (Northbridge Health)

Summary (auto)
- Discussed audit logging, SOC 2, and data retention options.
- Customer asked about reducing retention for troubleshooting artifacts.

Transcript
[00:00] Ben Carter: Cool, thanks everyone for joining. Quick overview, Redwood does hosted inference and we also have private deployments.
[00:32] Samantha Yuen: Our main questions are around PHI. Do you store prompts or responses?
[01:05] Priya Natarajan: We don't use customer data to train. For hosted, we have limited retention for debugging and abuse prevention, and access is controlled.
[02:10] Miguel Ortega: Right. Were not necessarily asking for zero, but we want the minimum. Whats the shortest ...
